mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp within 3 months, invalidating the recessionary pricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, validating earnings growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect immediate manufacturing and utility output growth, providing short-term earnings momentum that pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day returns during tightening cycles, with a negative or null Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated policy rates combined with a weakening labor market create a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and triggers risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined threshold condition does not precede negative 60-day returns in > 70% of historical occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth provides a direct measure of aggregate economic expansion, anchoring baseline corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and equity valuations, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual labor signal fails to predict positive returns when either series moves inversely, or the interaction term loses predictive significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 230 signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 230 do not precede negative 20-day returns in > 60% of observed cases, or the threshold effect shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.
